subject: [PATCH v3 2/2] x86/tdx: Fix zero-extension for 32-bit port I/O

reviews.author: Edgecombe, Rick P
reviews.summary: Recommended merging the patch despite some risk of complications due to ongoing discussions about the GHCI spec. Suggested that the patch is reasonable but may need adjustments based on future clarifications.

reviews.author: Dave Hansen
reviews.summary: Proposed a code-based solution to simplify the handle_in() function by using a switch statement instead of complex masking operations. Suggested that this approach would reduce comments and improve maintainability.

2026-05-27.patch_summary: This patch series addresses a bug in the TDX (Trusted Domain eXtensions) module where the handle_in() function does not properly zero-extend the result of 32-bit port I/O operations to 64 bits. The fix involves updating the handle_in() function to clear the entire RAX register when the I/O size is 4 bytes, ensuring correct zero-extension.
